authors: Amirdelfan K, Yu C, Doust MW, Gliner BE, Morgan DM, Kapural L, Vallejo R, Sitzman BT, Yearwood TL, Bundschu R, Yang T, Benyamin R, Burgher AH, Brooks ES, Powell AA, Subbaroyan J
title: Long-term quality of life improvement for chronic intractable back and leg pain patients using spinal cord stimulation: 12-month results from the SENZA-RCT
study_question: Does 10 kHz SCS improve quality of life more than traditional SCS in chronic back and leg pain?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic back and leg pain randomized to 10 kHz or traditional SCS at 10 US centers.
outcome_measures: ODI, GAF, SF-MPQ-2, PSQI, SF-12, GIC, remote programmer usage
inclusion_criteria: Chronic back and leg pain; successful trial with at least 40% VAS pain relief before implant.
answer_to_study_question: 10 kHz SCS improved disability, function, sleep, pain, and quality-of-life outcomes versus traditional SCS at 12 months.
reasoning: Anthropic and OpenRouter strongly agreed on bibliographic fields and participant count. I used consensus wording, keeping Gemini only where it plausibly added measures.
